reasoning_model_1: In analyzing the generated speech for Model 1, the model fails to correctly interpret the paralinguistic instruction '带着咂嘴说' (Say with smacking lips) and the text segment '咂嘴！'. At timestamp 00:00, the model explicitly reads the characters '咂嘴' (Za Zui) aloud as spoken words. This is a literal interpretation of the script where the stage direction describing the sound is treated as content to be read. Consequently, the model fails to produce the actual non-verbal vocalization (sound effect) required by the '非语言发声' feature, resulting in a failure to meet the core requirement of the prompt.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 correctly identifies the instruction and the nature of the text '咂嘴！' as a paralinguistic marker rather than spoken content. The transcript shows that the model begins the sentence '这杯特调...' at 00:00 without vocalizing the characters 'Za Zui'. This indicates that the model successfully converted the textual description into the intended non-verbal behavior (a lip-smacking sound or the appropriate pause/action) instead of reading it. The flow of the subsequent speech is natural, and the control over the non-verbal feature is correctly exercised by not treating the prompt instruction as script.
model_comparison: The primary difference lies in the interpretation of the command '咂嘴！' (Smack lips!). Model 1 (T1) failed the feature control test by vocalizing the text 'Za Zui' at 00:00, which contradicts the instruction to use a non-verbal sound. It treated the sound description as part of the sentence to be read. Model 2 (T2), in contrast, correctly refrained from speaking the text 'Za Zui' and integrated the feature as a non-verbal event (or omission of text in favor of the action), starting the speech '这杯...' directly. Model 2 demonstrates superior instruction following and feature control capabilities.
